title: fix: align UI SDK peer and React test deps
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Bumps `@pollinations/ui` to `0.0.2` and updates its optional `@pollinations/sdk` peer to `^5.0.0`.
- Aligns SDK React dev/test dependencies to React 19 so UI package builds/tests do not pull a React 18 test tree.
- Updates the root lockfile to dedupe the SDK/UI package React test dependency tree.

Notes:
- The SDK runtime React peer remains `^18.2.0 || ^19.0.0`; this changes only SDK dev/test dependencies.
- `packages/sdk/src/react/hooks.test.tsx` passes under React 19. React 19 emits `react-test-renderer` deprecation and `act(...)` environment warnings during that test.

Checks:
- `npx biome check --write packages/sdk/package.json packages/ui/package.json package-lock.json`
- `npm run build --prefix packages/sdk`
- `npm run test --prefix packages/sdk`
- `npm run build --prefix packages/ui`
- `npm run test --prefix packages/ui`

gist.headline: Spring Cleaning the SDK
gist.blurb: We've aligned the React test dependencies across our UI and SDK packages to keep our internal builds clean and happy.
gist.summary: Aligned the SDK's React dev and test dependencies to React 19 and bumped @pollinations/ui to 0.0.2 with an updated SDK peer dependency.
gist.impact: This dedupes the React test dependency tree and ensures UI package builds don't accidentally pull in React 18, keeping our internal tooling consistent.
gist.visual_concept: The bee organizing boxes or tools (representing packages/dependencies) in a cozy workshop. A React logo (atom symbol) on one of the boxes represents the React 19 alignment.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, organizing glowing blue atom-symbol boxes on a wooden shelf in a warm workshop. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
